name: Can I sue my employer directly if they ignored OSHA rules leading to my arm injury?
description: Usually no, due to 'exclusive remedy' laws. However, OSHA violations can be used to increase your benefits or prove a case against a third-party manufacturer.

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my employer directly if they ignored OSHA rules leading to my arm injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually no, due to 'exclusive remedy' laws. However, OSHA violations can be used to increase your benefits or prove a case against a third-party manufacturer.
acceptedAnswer.description: Directly suing your employer for OSHA violations in Boise is difficult because of Idaho's 'exclusive remedy' laws. Even if your boss was grossly negligent or ignored safety rules, workers' comp is usually your only recourse against them. The exception is if the employer acted with 'willful' or 'intentional' intent to harm you, which is extremely rare and hard to prove. However, OSHA violations are incredible evidence for a third-party claim against a manufacturer or building owner. Furthermore, in some jurisdictions, an employer's safety violation can lead to a percentage increase in your workers' comp award. A lawyer will use OSHA's investigation report to build a much stronger case for maximum disability benefits.
